title: zieria arborescens subsp. glabrifolia
intro: Zieria arborescens subsp. glabrifolia, also known as the Glabrous Zieria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. It is a shrub that produces small, white flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Zieria arborescens subsp. glabrifolia can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
